prose: HOUSE uses both flat hands, fingertips touching to form the shape of a peaked roof, then the hands move apart and down to trace the walls. Palms face each other for the roof, then palms face down for the walls.

gaps.reason: the prose never anchors the sign to a body location — the hands appear to be in neutral space but it is not stated explicitly
gaps.suggested_question: Is HOUSE performed in neutral space in front of the chest, or anchored to a specific body location?

gaps.reason: description mentions only a peaked roof shape, not where the fingertips point (up-forward would be typical but is not said)
gaps.suggested_question: During the roof shape, do the fingertips point up, up-forward, or toward each other?

notes: Two mandatory slots silent; palm direction described only for first phase.
